title: Agent Behavior Scenario v1
description:
A single agent-behavior test scenario — the non-deterministic-verification unit consumed by the LSP Brains agent-behavior scoring domain (spec §15). Authored as YAML or JSON; loaded by the `abv-run` harness. Each scenario runs one or more independent trials; each trial is prompt → response → judge → feedback. Gold samples calibrate the judge; they are NOT trials.

See spec §15 (Agent Behavior Verification) for the full authoring contract. See `agent-behavior-result-v1.schema.json` for what a run of this scenario produces.

id.description: Kebab-case identifier, unique within the scenario library. E.g. 'lsp-code-optimality', 'hat-discipline'.

version.description: Scenario schema + rubric version. Bump when the rubric or prompt changes in a way that invalidates prior scores. Runs against different versions should not be compared.

target.description: What agent-behavior facet this scenario tests.

kind.description: The class of behavior under test. 'general' for cross-cutting behaviors. 'skill:<name>' scopes to a specific skill file. 'hat:<name>' scopes to a declared hat. 'culture:<invariant>' scopes to one of the five culture invariants (positivity / integrity / honesty / critical_but_kind / respect).

scope.description: One-sentence description of WHEN the measured behavior should apply. Informative; not machine-enforced. Human readers use this to decide if a scenario applies to their agent.

prompt.description: The user-impersonation prompt sent to the agent-under-test. No templating in v1 — plain string. Authors should avoid secrets / PII in prompts (the Anthropic API sees the literal text).

system_prompt.description: Optional override for the system prompt sent to the agent-under-test. When omitted, the harness composes one from the target agent's skills + hats + culture.yaml.

rubric.description: The grading criteria. Total weights across the rubric typically sum to 100 but are not required to. Judge returns a per-criterion score (0-weight) and the per-trial total is the sum, clamped to [0, 100].

id.description: Snake-case identifier, unique within this scenario.

weight.description: Maximum points this criterion contributes. Integer so judge output is unambiguous.

description.description: Prose describing the behavior the judge should look for. Must be specific enough that two humans scoring the same response would agree within 10 points.

trials.description: Number of independent trials to run per scenario execution. Non-determinism means a single trial isn't meaningful; 3-5 catches egregious cases, 10+ gives tighter distributions at proportional cost.

pass_threshold.description: Per-trial score threshold for a trial to count as 'passing'. Scenario-level pass is a majority of trials passing.

judge_model.description: Anthropic model id used to score responses. 'claude-haiku-*' for sandbox/CI; 'claude-sonnet-*' or 'claude-opus-*' for release-gate runs.

agent_model.description: Optional override for the model backing the agent-under-test. When omitted, harness default (from claude-proxy config) is used.

timeout_seconds.description: Per-trial wall-clock timeout. Exceeded trials log `result: error` and are excluded from aggregate scoring (though the error itself surfaces in the CMDB findings).

feedback_prompt.description: Optional override for the feedback-elicitation prompt sent to the agent-under-test after scoring. When omitted, the harness uses a default template that includes the score, rubric findings, and explanation. Supports `{score}`, `{findings}`, `{explanation}` placeholders.

skip_feedback.description: When true, the feedback elicitation step is skipped. Useful for cheap smoke runs or scenarios where feedback isn't informative (e.g., the harness canary).

gold_samples.description: Calibration fixtures: known-good and known-bad responses with the score a human reviewer assigned. The gold-sample test asserts the judge scores within ±10 of the human label. Frozen — edits to a gold sample require a rubric version bump.

label.description: Category tag. `gold-good` = human scored ≥ 80. `gold-bad` = human scored ≤ 40. `gold-edge` = ambiguous cases near the threshold.

human_score.description: Score a human reviewer assigned after reading the response against this scenario's rubric.

human_notes.description: Optional reviewer notes about why the response earned that score. Not used by the judge; retained for humans debugging judge drift.

response.description: The (pre-recorded) agent response being scored. Plays the role of step 3 in the trial loop, without actually running the agent.
